table.name: Launch Control RPM Delta

table.name: Engine Load Limiter A
table.description: Engine load limit against RPM.

table.name: Engine Load Limiter B
table.description: Engine load limit against RPM.

table.name: MAF Voltage Limits (CEL)
table.description: When MAF sensor voltage is greater than or equal to the first value or less than the second value, over a specific period of time, a CEL will be triggered. The time delay is determined by the 'MAF Sensor CEL Delay' table.

table.name: MAF Limit (Maximum)
table.description: This is the maximum airflow that will be used by the ECU. Airflow will be capped at this limit regardless of the airflow values in the 'MAF Sensor Scaling' table.

table.name: MAF Sensor Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling for the mass airflow sensor.

table.name: 5V Sensor Scaling Table 3
table.description: This is the scaling for the mass airflow sensor.

table.name: 5V Sensor Scaling Table 4
table.description: This is the scaling for the mass airflow sensor.

table.name: 5V Sensor Scaling Table 5
table.description: This is the scaling for the mass airflow sensor.

table.name: MAF Compensation (IAT)
table.description: This is the compensation of airflow based on intake temp.

table.name: Engine Load Limit (Maximum)
table.description: This is the maximum allowable engine load. Engine load will be capped at this limit regardless of actual engine load.

table.name: Engine Load Compensation (MP)
table.description: This is the compensation of engine load based on RPM and manifold pressure.

table.name: Engine Load Multiplier
table.description: Multiplier for engine load calculation.

table.name: Primary Open Loop Fueling A
table.description: This fuel map is used in open loop when the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) is greater than or equal to the threshold specified by the 'Primary Open Loop Fuel Map Switch (IAM)' table. Because there is no feedback in open loop, the actual AFR may differ from the values presented in this table. In addition, the ECU applies a long-term correction (A/F Learning) to open loop fueling from patterns it recognizes in closed loop fueling. Other compensations and minimum enrichment factors exist as well. Because the underlying values of this table are enrichment offsets relative to stoichiometric, AFRs leaner than 14.7, as presented, are not valid.

table.name: Primary Open Loop Fueling B
table.description: This fuel map is used in open loop when the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) is greater than or equal to the threshold specified by the 'Primary Open Loop Fuel Map Switch (IAM)' table. Because there is no feedback in open loop, the actual AFR may differ from the values presented in this table. In addition, the ECU applies a long-term correction (A/F Learning) to open loop fueling from patterns it recognizes in closed loop fueling. Other compensations and minimum enrichment factors exist as well. Because the underlying values of this table are enrichment offsets relative to stoichiometric, AFRs leaner than 14.7, as presented, are not valid.

table.name: Primary Open Loop Fueling Minimum
table.description: A certain fueling additive (considering ECT) calculation normally ramps up the additive gradually (ranging from +0.00 to +0.40); however, when throttle is maxed (> 85), the 2019 S10C open loop code seems to (approximately) enforce a minimum threshold before the open loop calculations.

table.name: Primary Open Loop Fueling Additive A
table.description: This fuel map is used as an additive to the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling Base' map when the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) is less than 1.0. An estimated AFR for final primary open loop fueling can be calculated as follows: 14.7/(((14.7/(Base AFR map value)) + (Additive map value * (1.0 - current IAM))). For example, if the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling Base' calls for an effective AFR of 10.5:1 and the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling Additive' map calls for 0.10 enrichment offset compensation and the current IAM is 0.75, then the final primary ol fueling would have an estimated AFR of 10.3:1.

table.name: Primary Open Loop Fueling Additive B
table.description: This fuel map is used as an additive to the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling Base' map when the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) is less than 1.0. An estimated AFR for final primary open loop fueling can be calculated as follows: 14.7/(((14.7/(Base AFR map value)) + (Additive map value * (1.0 - current IAM))). For example, if the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling Base' calls for an effective AFR of 10.5:1 and the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling Additive' map calls for 0.10 enrichment offset compensation and the current IAM is 0.75, then the final primary ol fueling would have an estimated AFR of 10.3:1.

table.name: Minimum Active Primary Open Loop Enrichment
table.description: This is the minimum enrichment (leanest estimated AFR) for active primary open loop fueling. This threshold is compared to the enrichment as determined by the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling' table which is compensated by the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling Compensation (Timing Compensation)' table.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation (Load)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on load and engine speed. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied. Note: Lean compensation in this table will potentially force open loop during normally closed loop fueling conditions.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation (Load)(MT)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on load and engine speed. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied. Note: Lean compensation in this table will potentially force open loop during normally closed loop fueling conditions.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation (Load)(AT)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on load and engine speed. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied. Note: Lean compensation in this table will potentially force open loop during normally closed loop fueling conditions.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation A (Load)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on load and engine speed. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied. Note: Lean compensation in this table will potentially force open loop during normally closed loop fueling conditions.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation B (Load)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on load and engine speed. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied. Note: Lean compensation in this table will potentially force open loop during normally closed loop fueling conditions.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation C (Load)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on load and engine speed. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied. Note: Lean compensation in this table will potentially force open loop during normally closed loop fueling conditions.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation D (Load)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on load and engine speed. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied. Note: Lean compensation in this table will potentially force open loop during normally closed loop fueling conditions.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation Imm. Cruise (ECT)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on coolant temp. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). Note: This is based on the immediate conditions related to cruise/non-cruise and results in an immediate switch, not the ramping behavior inherent with other tables when switching.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation Imm. Non-Cruise (ECT)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on coolant temp. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). Note: This is based on the immediate conditions related to cruise/non-cruise and results in an immediate switch, not the ramping behavior inherent with other tables when switching.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation (ECT)
table.description: This is the compensation to the closed loop base fueling target based on coolant temp. Other compensations (some undefined), are also applied.

table.name: CL Fueling Target Compensation (ECT) Disable
table.description: When coolant temp is greater than or equal to this value, the 'CL Fueling Target Compensation (ECT)' is no longer applied.

table.name: CL to OL Delay
table.description: This is the period over which the 'CL to OL with Delay' throttle or base pulse width thresholds must be continuously exceeded before the closed loop to open loop fueling transition can take place. Only one of the values is used at any given time to determine the delay. If the current delay is non-zero, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' or 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' tables will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop if either threshold is continuously exceeded over the current delay period. If the delay is zero, then these tables will not be used and the closed loop to open loop transition will be decided by the current enrichment as determined by the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling' and 'Minimum Active Primary Open Loop Enrichment' tables.

table.name: CL to OL Delay
table.description: This delay value is used for all SI-DRIVE modes if the 'CL to OL Delay/Switch SI-DRIVE Intelligent' value is zero, otherwise, in SI-DRIVE intelligent mode ONLY, the latter delay is used. The delay value is the period over which the 'CL to OL with Delay' throttle or base pulse width thresholds must be continuously exceeded before the closed loop to open loop fueling transition can take place. Only one of the values is used at any given time to determine the delay. If the current delay is non-zero, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' or 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' tables will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop if either threshold is continuously exceeded over the current delay period. If the delay is zero, then these tables will not be used and the closed loop to open loop transition will be decided by the current enrichment as determined by the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling' and 'Minimum Active Primary Open Loop Enrichment' tables.

table.name: CL to OL Delay/Switch SI-DRIVE Intelligent
table.description: When this table's value is non-zero, it is used as the current delay when the SI-DRIVE Intelligent mode is active. When this value is zero, the delay determined by the 'CL to OL Delay' table will be used, regardless of SI-DRIVE mode. The delay is the period over which the 'CL to OL with Delay' throttle or base pulse width thresholds must be continuously exceeded before the closed loop to open loop fueling transition can take place. Only one of the delay table values is used at any given time to determine the delay. If the current delay is non-zero, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' or 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' tables will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop if either threshold is continuously exceeded over the current delay period. If the delay is zero, then these tables will not be used and the closed loop to open loop transition will be decided by the current enrichment as determined by the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling' and 'Minimum Active Primary Open Loop Enrichment' tables.

table.name: CL to OL Delay (Atm. Pressure)
table.description: This is the period over which the 'CL to OL with Delay' throttle or base pulse width thresholds must be continuously exceeded before the closed loop to open loop fueling transition can take place. Only one of these delay values will be used depending on atmospheric pressure. If the current delay is non-zero, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' or 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' tables will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop if either threshold is continuously exceeded over the current delay period. If the delay is zero, then these tables will not be used and the closed loop to open loop transition will be decided by the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling' map.

table.name: CL to OL Delay A (Atm. Pressure)
table.description: This is the period over which the 'CL to OL with Delay' throttle or base pulse width thresholds must be continuously exceeded before the closed loop to open loop fueling transition can take place. Only one of these delay values will be used depending on atmospheric pressure. If the current delay is non-zero, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' or 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' tables will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop if either threshold is continuously exceeded over the current delay period. If the delay is zero, then these tables will not be used and the closed loop to open loop transition will be decided by the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling' map.

table.name: CL to OL Delay B (Atm. Pressure)
table.description: This is the period over which the 'CL to OL with Delay' throttle or base pulse width thresholds must be continuously exceeded before the closed loop to open loop fueling transition can take place. Only one of these delay values will be used depending on atmospheric pressure. If the current delay is non-zero, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' or 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' tables will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop if either threshold is continuously exceeded over the current delay period. If the delay is zero, then these tables will not be used and the closed loop to open loop transition will be decided by the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Primary Open Loop Fueling' map.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When throttle position is equal to or rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). used to determine the pause in this transition to open loop. When throttle position drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay (Accelerator)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When accelerator pedal opening % is equal to or rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). When accelerator pedal opening % drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay A (Throttle)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When throttle position is equal to or rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). When throttle position drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay B (Throttle)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When throttle position is equal to or rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). When throttle position drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay C (Throttle)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When throttle position is equal to or rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). When throttle position drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay Throttle Hysteresis
OL to CL Transition
table.data: Hysteresis Below 'CL to OL Transition with Delay Throttle' Map Value

table.description: When throttle position is equal to or less than this hysteresis subtracted from the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay Throttle' map value, the potential transition from open loop to closed loop begins (dependent on the primary open loop fuel map value and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay Load' threshold).

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When the base pulse width, ((2707.09/Injector Flow Scaling) * Engine Load (g/rev))), rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). When the base pulse width drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay A (Base Pulse Width)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When the base pulse width, ((2707.09/Injector Flow Scaling) * Engine Load (g/rev))), rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). When the base pulse width drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay B (Base Pulse Width)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When the base pulse width, ((2707.09/Injector Flow Scaling) * Engine Load (g/rev))), rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). When the base pulse width drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay C (Base Pulse Width)
table.description: When the closed loop delay value is non-zero, this table will be used to determine the transition from closed loop to open loop and back again. When the base pulse width, ((2707.09/Injector Flow Scaling) * Engine Load (g/rev))), rises above the threshold in this table, the process to exit closed loop begins. The current delay value is a counter threshold for which the throttle threshold must be continuously exceeded (otherwise counter is reset to zero and CL to OL transition does not take place). When the base pulse width drops below the threshold (and below a predetermined delta), fueling will transition from open loop to closed loop.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay BPW Hysteresis
OL to CL Transition
table.data: Hysteresis Below 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (BPW)' Map Value

table.description: When the base pulse width is equal to or less than this hysteresis subtracted from the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' map value, the potential transition from open loop to closed loop begins (dependent on the primary open loop fuel map value and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay Throttle' threshold)

table.name: CL to OL Transition Counter Step Value (MAF)

scaling.units: CL to OL Counter Increment (WARNING - value should NEVER be zero)

table.description: This value determines the increment of the CL to OL transition counter based on MAF. This counter is incremented when the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay' load or throttle thresholds are continuously exceeded. When the counter is greater than or equal to the current delay value, the transition from CL to OL will occur (depending on the fuel map). WARNING - this value should NEVER be zero.

table.name: CL to OL Transition with Delay (Injector Load Threshold)
table.description: Injector load threshold, varying with RPM, above which fuelling will go open loop after a delay.

table.name: CL Delay Period
table.description: Delay periods when changing from closed to open loop fuelling. Reduce these for quicker open loop operation.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum (EGT)
table.description: When the EGT is the same or greater than the second value, the closed loop Delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When the EGT drops below the first value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum (Throttle)
table.description: When throttle position is greater than or equal to this value, the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When throttle position is less than this value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Maximum Vehicle Speed
table.description: When vehicle speed is the same or greater than the second value, the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When vehicle speed drops below the first value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, depending on the delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Minimum (ECT)
table.description: When coolant temp is the less than this value, the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When coolant temp is greater than or equal to this value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum (Engine Load)
(Condition) - Result
table.data: (Above) - Clear CL Delay if Load Counter Threshold Exceeded

table.description: When the engine load is the same or greater than the second value, a counter value is incremented. If engine load remains equal to greater than the second value, the counter will be continue to be incremented and if it exceeds the 'CL Delay Engine Load Counter Threshold' value, the primary closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When engine load drops below the first value, the engine load counter value is set to zero and other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Engine Load Counter Threshold
table.description: This is the delay in clearing the primary closed loop delay value if engine load is greater than or equal to the value determined by the 'CL Delay Maximum (Engine Load)' table.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed (Per Gear)
table.description: When engine speed is the same or greater than the second value (by gear), the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When engine speed drops below the first value (by gear), other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed (Per Gear)(MT)
table.description: When engine speed is the same or greater than the second value (by gear), the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When engine speed drops below the first value (by gear), other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed (Per Gear)(AT)
table.description: When engine speed is the same or greater than the second value (by gear), the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When engine speed drops below the first value (by gear), other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed (Neutral)
table.description: This table is used when the current gear is not being determined by the ECU, such as neutral. When engine speed is the same or greater than the second value, the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When engine speed drops below the first value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed A
table.description: When engine speed is the same or greater than the second value, the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When engine speed drops below the first value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed B
(Condition) - Result
table.data: (Above) - Clear CL Delay if Engine Speed Counter Threshold Exceeded

table.description: When engine speed is the same or greater than the second value, a counter value is incremented. If engine speed remains equal to greater than the second value, the counter will be continue to be incremented and if it exceeds the 'CL Delay Engine Speed B Counter Threshold' value, the primary closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When engine speed drops below the first value, the counter value is set to zero and other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Engine Speed B Counter Threshold
table.description: This is the delay in clearing the primary closed loop delay value if engine speed is greater than or equal to the value determined by the 'CL Delay Maximum Engine Speed B' table.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum (Throttle) A
(Condition) - Result
table.data: (Above) - Clear CL Delay if Throttle Counter Threshold Exceeded

table.description: When the accelerator pedal opening % is the same or greater than the second value, a counter value is incremented. If the accelerator pedal opening % remains equal to greater than the second value, the counter will be continue to be incremented and if it exceeds the 'Closed Loop Delay (Accelerator Pedal)' value, the primary closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When the accelerator pedal opening % drops below the first value, the counter value is set to zero and other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Throttle A Counter Threshold
table.description: This is the delay in clearing the primary closed loop delay value if accelerator pedal opening % is greater than or equal to the value determined by the 'Closed Loop Accelerator Pedal' table.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum (Throttle) B (ECT)
table.description: When the accelerator pedal opening % is the same or greater than the second value, the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When the accelerator pedal opening % below the first value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, depending on the delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Throttle Atm. Pressure Thresholds
table.description: If atmospheric pressure is equal to or exceeds the first value, then the 'CL Delay Maximum (Throttle) (Low Atmospheric Pressure)' table is used. If it is below the second value, the 'CL Delay Maximum (Throttle) (High Atmospheric Pressure)' table is used.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum (Throttle) (Low Atm. Pressure)
table.description: When throttle position is greater than or equal to the selected value in this table, the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When throttle position is less than the selected value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: CL Delay Maximum (Throttle) (High Atm. Pressure)
table.description: When throttle position is greater than or equal to the selected value in this table, the closed loop delay value is set to zero which can result in switching from closed loop to open loop depending on the current enrichment value as determined by the 'Open Loop Fueling' map. When throttle position is less than the selected value, other specific closed loop triggers are reviewed. If all these triggers are below their thresholds, then the closed loop delay is determined from the 'CL to OL Delay' table. In this case, assuming a non-zero delay value, the 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Base Pulse Width)' and 'CL to OL Transition with Delay (Throttle)' are used to determine the open loop to close loop transition and vice versa.

table.name: Injector Latency
table.description: Injector latency (dead-time)

table.name: Injector Flow Scaling
table.description: This is the fuel injector constant represented with an estimated flow rating (gas only). The underlying raw value does NOT represent the injector flow rate and there is no standard for measuring the flow rate of injectors. Therefore, it should NOT be thought of as a value that is going to exactly match published rates for your injectors but as a means to get you in the general ball park as a starting point to tune from.

table.name: Injector Flow Scaling BRZ
table.description: This is the fuel injector constant represented with an estimated flow rating (gas only). The underlying raw value does NOT represent the injector flow rate and there is no standard for measuring the flow rate of injectors. Therefore, it should NOT be thought of as a value that is going to exactly match published rates for your injectors but as a means to get you in the general ball park as a starting point to tune from.

table.name: Port Injection MRP Compensation
table.description: Multiplier for PI time. This map can be used to proportionally increase the PI (Port Injection) volume amount. This should be calibrated above 0.00bar when forced induction is added so the PI fuel delivery volume is correct for a given boost pressure.

table.name: Minimum Port Injection Quantity
table.description: Minimum allowed fuel volume from the port injectors when they are enabled.

table.name: Injector Minimum Open Time
table.description: The operating frequency of the solenoid valve. On turbo conversions where the CPC valve is replaced with a fast switching wastegate duty solenoid valve this will vary depending on the type used.

table.name: Per Injector Primary Fuel Offset Compensation A
table.description: This determines the additive (per injector) to the base injector duration multiplier based on the last calculated injector pulse width and engine speed. The base injector duration is the injector pulse width necessary for stoich fueling at the current engine load. The base injector duration multiplier determines the correction applied to achieve a desired level of enrichment (or enleanment). No enrichment or enleanment would result in a base injector duration multiplier of 1.0. The offset from this table is added to other factors (such as primary open loop enrichment) and then added to this base injector duration multiplier to achieve the desired level of enrichment or enleanment. To estimate the effect of this compensation, first estimate the desired AFR that the compensation would be applied to (ex. 12:1 AFR). Convert this to the base injector duration multiplier (ex. 14.7/x = 14.7/12 = 1.225). Add the per injector compensation to this multiplier (ex. 1.225 + 0.05 = 1.275). Then convert the multiplier back to the estimated AFR (ex. 14.7/x = 14.7/1.275 = 11.53 AFR). It is not currently known which table corresponds to which injector. WARNING: UNTESTED

table.name: Per Injector Primary Fuel Offset Compensation B
table.description: This determines the additive (per injector) to the base injector duration multiplier based on the last calculated injector pulse width and engine speed. The base injector duration is the injector pulse width necessary for stoich fueling at the current engine load. No enrichment or enleanment would result in a base injector duration multiplier of 1.0. table.name: Per Injector Pulse Width Compensation A
table.description: This determines the compensation (per injector) to the current calculated injector duration based on the last calculated injector duration and engine speed. The calculated injector pulse width is based on engine load and a number of other correction factors necessary to achieve the desired fueling. It is not currently known which table corresponds to which injector. WARNING: UNTESTED

table.name: Low Pulse Width Fuel Injector Compensation
table.description: This compensation is applied to the injector pulsewidth when the RPM and IPW thresholds are met. It is used to compensate for nonlinear injector response at low pulsewidth. This portion of the response is known as the 'knee'

table.name: Low Pulse Width Fuel Injector Compensation Maximum RPM
table.description: This is the maximum RPM that the Low Pulse Width Fuel Injector Compensation is applied

table.name: Low Pulse Width Fuel Injector Compensation Maximum IPW
table.description: This is the maximum RPM that the Low Pulse Width Fuel Injector Compensation is applied

table.name: Direct Injection Quantity Maximum
table.description: Maximum allowed quantity of DI fuel delivery when the DI system is active.

table.name: Direct Injection Quantity Minimum
table.description: no description

table.name: GDI Firing Angle Cold Idle (Override)
table.description: When enabled, modify the ECU code pointer for the Cold Idle table to refer to the High Load Cold table. See also ZA1JS20G.

state.name: Override Cold Idle Table location to High Load Cold Table
Use OEM Cold Idle Table location

table.name: GDI Firing Angle High Load Hot (Override)
table.description: When enabled, modify the ECU code pointer for the High Load Hot table to refer to the High Load Cold table. See also ZA1JS20G.

state.name: Override High Load Hot Table location to High Load Cold Table
Use OEM High Load Hot Table location

table.name: GDI Firing Angle Low Load Cold (Override)
table.description: When enabled, modify the ECU code pointer for the Low Load Cold table to refer to the High Load Cold table. See also ZA1JS20G.

state.name: Override Low Load Cold Table location to High Load Cold Table
Use OEM Low Load Cold Table location

table.name: GDI Firing Angle Low Load Hot (Override)
table.description: When enabled, modify the ECU code pointer for the Low Load Hot table to refer to the High Load Cold table. See also ZA1JS20G.

state.name: Override Low Load Hot Table location to High Load Cold Table
Use OEM Low Load Hot Table location

table.name: GDI Firing Angle Cold Idle
table.description: no description

table.name: GDI Firing Angle High Load Cold
table.description: no description

table.name: GDI Firing Angle High Load Hot
table.description: no description

table.name: GDI Firing Angle Low Load Cold
table.description: no description

table.name: GDI Firing Angle Low Load Hot
table.description: no description

table.name: GDI Pressure Multiplier A
table.description: This map is a compensation table to adjust the quantity of DI fuel injected against fuel rail pressure.

table.name: GDI Pressure Multiplier B
table.description: This map is a compensation table to adjust the quantity of DI fuel injected against fuel rail pressure.

table.name: GDI Pressure Multiplier C
table.description: Multiplies GDI Pressure Multipliers A and B before passing value to GDI Flow Table.

table.name: GDI Pressure Target A
table.description: This is the requested fuel pressure that is supplied to the DI system, the DI fuel pump is a mechanical unit and therefore has limitations as to what it can deliver.

table.name: GDI Pressure Target B
table.description: This is the requested fuel pressure that is supplied to the DI system, the DI fuel pump is a mechanical unit and therefore has limitations as to what it can deliver.

table.name: Total Injection Ratio Port Cold
table.description: Controls the ratio of port injection (PI) vs direct injection (DI) to deliver the requested volume of fuel. The value in the map is the proportion of PI quantity. 100% means 100% PI and the DI system is never used,.

table.name: Total Injection Ratio Port Warm
table.description: Controls the ratio of port injection (PI) vs direct injection (DI) to deliver the requested volume of fuel. The value in the map is the proportion of PI quantity. 100% means 100% PI and the DI system is never used,.

table.name: Total Injection Ratio Port Hot
table.description: Controls the ratio of port injection (PI) vs direct injection (DI) to deliver the requested volume of fuel. The value in the map is the proportion of PI quantity. 100% means 100% PI and the DI system is never used,.

table.name: Total Injection Ratio Port Cold (Override)
table.description: When enabled, modify the ECU code pointer for the Cold table to refer to the Warm table. See also ZA1JS20G.

state.name: Override Cold Table location to Warm Table

table.name: Total Injection Ratio Port Hot (Override)
table.description: When enabled, modify the ECU code pointer for the Hot table to refer to the Warm table. See also ZA1JS20G.

state.name: Override Hot Table location to Warm Table

table.name: PI Ratio ECT Compensation (Additive)
table.description: Result = (Additive[ECT] + Load[ECT, Load]) * Compensation[ECT, Total Injection Ratio Port]

table.name: PI Ratio ECT Compensation (Load)
table.description: Result = (Additive[ECT] + Load[ECT, Load]) * Compensation[ECT, Total Injection Ratio Port]

table.name: PI Ratio ECT Compensation
table.description: Result = (Additive[ECT] + Load[ECT, Load]) * Compensation[ECT, Total Injection Ratio Port]

table.name: PI Ratio ECT Unknown
table.description: A certain memory location is set to true if the PI ratio's ECT result in this table is greater than the other ECT Unknown table's result.

table.name: ECT Unknown
table.description: A certain memory location is set to true if the PI ratio's ECT result in the other table is greater than this table's ECT result. This table appears to be used by non-PI functions, but is included here as it influences DI/PI.

table.name: ECT Unknown B
table.description: A certain memory location is set to true if the PI ratio's ECT result in the other table is greater than this table's ECT result. This table appears to be used by non-PI functions, but is included here as it influences DI/PI.

table.name: PI Ratio Thresholds
table.description: If PI Ratio RPM and Load Thresholds are not exceeded, PI Ratio is set to 0% if this value is not exceeded

table.name: PI Ratio Threshold (RPM)
table.description: If this RPM threshold is exceeded, PI Ratio full on and full off thresholds are not applied

table.name: PI Ratio Threshold (Load)
table.description: If this Load threshold is exceeded, PI Ratio full on and full off thresholds are not applied

table.name: PI Ratio Threshold (Unknown)
table.description: Under certain conditions, the threshold is not calculated and this static value is used instead. (S20G- if FFF8: A286 false, A220 < 0x30, and BEE0 or BECD false)

table.name: Throttle Tip-in Enrichment
table.description: This is the additional enrichment during throttle tip-in. Throttle tip-in is the difference between the current throttle position and the last throttle position. This enrichment represents an additional and separate firing of the injectors. The larger the value, the more fuel is potentially added. Tip-in Enrichment is not active if the thresholds, as determined by the 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation' and 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation (Throttle)' tables, are not met as well as other undefined thresholds.

table.name: Throttle Tip-in Enrichment A
table.description: This is the additional enrichment during throttle tip-in. Throttle tip-in is the difference between the current throttle position and the last throttle position. This enrichment represents an additional and separate firing of the injectors. The larger the value, the more fuel is potentially added. Tip-in Enrichment is not active if the thresholds, as determined by the 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation' and 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation (Throttle)' tables, are not met as well as other undefined thresholds.

table.name: Throttle Tip-in Enrichment B
table.description: This is the additional enrichment during throttle tip-in. Throttle tip-in is the difference between the current throttle position and the last throttle position. This enrichment represents an additional and separate firing of the injectors. The larger the value, the more fuel is potentially added. Tip-in Enrichment is not active if the thresholds, as determined by the 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation' and 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation (Throttle)' tables, are not met as well as other undefined thresholds.

table.name: Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation
table.description: Tip-in Enrichment is not active until the calculated additional IPW, as determined by the 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' table and with compensations applied, exceeds this value. This table does not act independently and other requirements must also be met in order for tip-in enrichment to be active.

table.name: Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation (Throttle)
table.description: This is the minimum throttle tip-in for active tip-in enrichment. This table does not act independently and other requirements must also be met in order for tip-in enrichment to be active.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation (Boost Error)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on boost error (the difference between target boost and actual boost).

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation (RPM)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on engine speed.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation (MRP)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on manifold pressure.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation A (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation B (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation C (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation D (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation D (ECT) Activation
table.description: This is the minimum throttle tip-in for the 'Tip-in Enrichment D (ECT)' table to be active. This table does not act independently and other requirements must also be met in order for tip-in enrichment to be active.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation D (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation D (ECT) Activation
table.description: This is the minimum throttle tip-in for the 'Tip-in Enrichment D (ECT)' table to be active. This table does not act independently and other requirements must also be met in order for tip-in enrichment to be active.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Counter Threshold
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment counter is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is incremented each time tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or the threshold in the 'Tip-in Enrichment Applied Counter Reset' table is exceeded.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Counter Threshold A (ECT)
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment counter is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is incremented each time tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or the threshold in the 'Tip-in Enrichment Applied Counter Reset' table is exceeded.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Counter Threshold B (ECT)
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment counter is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is incremented each time tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or the threshold in the 'Tip-in Enrichment Applied Counter Reset' table is exceeded.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Applied Counter Reset
table.description: When the period between tip-in enrichment application exceeds the threshold in this table, the applied tip-in enrichment counter is cleared. The period between tip-in enrichment application is a counter that is cleared when tip-in enrichment is applied and incremented when the tip-in enrichment routine is executed. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is incremented each time tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or the threshold in this table is exceeded. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is the value that is compared to the 'Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Counter Threshold' for disabling tip-in enrichment.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Throttle Cumulative Threshold
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The current throttle tip-in is added to the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value when tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or when the last applied counter threshold exceeded the 'Tip-in Throttle Cumulative Reset' threshold.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Throttle Cumulative Threshold A (ECT)
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The current throttle tip-in is added to the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value when tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or when the last applied counter threshold exceeded the 'Tip-in Throttle Cumulative Reset' threshold.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Throttle Cumulative Threshold B (ECT)
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The current throttle tip-in is added to the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value when tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or when the last applied counter threshold exceeded the 'Tip-in Throttle Cumulative Reset' threshold.

table.name: Tip-in Throttle Cumulative Reset
table.description: When the period between tip-in enrichment application exceeds the threshold in this table, the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value is cleared. The period between tip-in enrichment application is a counter that is cleared when tip-in enrichment is applied and incremented when the tip-in enrichment routine is executed. The current throttle tip-in is added to the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value when tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or when the last applied counter threshold is exceeded in this table. The applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle is the value that is compared to the 'Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Throttle Cumulative Threshold' for disabling tip-in enrichment.

table.name: A/F Learning #1 Limits
table.description: These are the minimum and maximum ranges for A/F Learning #1. A/F Learning #1 is the long-term correction applied to fueling based on feedback from the oxygen sensor during closed loop operation.

table.name: A/F Learning #1 Airflow Ranges A
A/F Learning Store/Apply Ranges
table.data: Max Range A / Min Range B
Max Range B / Min Range C
Max Range C / Min Range D
Max Range D / Min Range E
Max Range E / Min Range F

table.description: These are the airflow ranges in which the different long-term fuel trims are calculated in closed loop and applied to the same airflow ranges for both closed loop and open loop.

table.name: A/F Learning #1 Airflow Ranges B
A/F Learning Store/Apply Ranges
table.data: Max Range A / Min Range B
Max Range B / Min Range C
Max Range C / Min Range D
Max Range D / Min Range E
Max Range E / Min Range F

table.description: These are the airflow ranges in which the different long-term fuel trims are calculated in closed loop and applied to the same airflow ranges for both closed loop and open loop.

table.name: A/F #3 CL Target Compensation Limits
table.description: Change in target boost in 1st gear at vehicle speeds below the 'Target Boost Compensation (1st Gear) Speed Disable' value.

table.name: A/F #3 Correction Limits
table.description: These limits determine the maximum AF #3 Correction (Rear O2 Sensor Feedback) applied during closed loop fueling. By setting them to zero you can disable the effect of the rear O2 sensor on closed loop fueling.

table.name: A/F #3 Learning Limits
table.description: These limits determine the maximum AF #3 Correction (Rear O2 Sensor Feedback) applied during closed loop fueling. By setting them to zero you can disable the effect of the rear O2 sensor on closed loop fueling.

table.name: Front Oxygen Sensor Scaling A
table.description: This is the scaling for the front oxygen sensor.

table.name: Front Oxygen Sensor Scaling B
table.description: This is the scaling for the front oxygen sensor.

table.name: Front Oxygen Sensor Rich Limit
table.description: This is the rich limit for the front oxygen sensor. Regardless of the scaling of the front oxygen sensor, it will not read richer than this value.

table.name: Front Oxygen Sensor Compensation (Atm. Pressure)
table.description: This is the compensation of the front oxygen sensor at different atmospheric pressures. Calculate the compensation as follows: ((Front O2 AFR - 14.7) x Compensation Value) + 14.7. Regardless of compensation, AFR will still be limited on the rich side by the 'Front Oxygen Sensor Rich Limit' table and limited to an AFR of 29.4 on the lean side.

table.name: Overrun Fueling RPM Resume Threshold
table.description: This 2D table determines (on an ECT x axis) at what RPM the injectors switch back on from coasting (i.e. full lean, and injector pulse width at zero). This would be useful to those with much larger injectors than stock that have a problem with stalling out as the vehicle comes to stop / idle.

table.name: Overrun Enrich RPM Delta Activation
table.description: This table sets the required RPM delta for overrun enrichment to activate.

table.name: Overrun initial injector enrichment (pulsewidth)
table.description: This table sets the initial overrun enrichment when active.

table.name: Overrun Fueling Cut Counter RPM Threshold
table.description: These counters determine the RPM count before injectors cut off during overrun.

table.name: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width A (ECT)
table.description: This is the injector pulse width based on coolant temp when cranking the motor. Compensation tables may impact the final pulse width. Although the exact conditions for map switching is not entirely known, some of it is based on whether the ignition switch is on or off and whether the motor has begun to start or not at any given time in the cranking process. TGV status may also be involved. WARNING: UNTESTED

table.name: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width B (ECT)
table.description: This is the injector pulse width based on coolant temp when cranking the motor. Compensation tables may impact the final pulse width. Although the exact conditions for map switching is not entirely known, some of it is based on whether the ignition switch is on or off and whether the motor has begun to start or not at any given time in the cranking process. table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation A (RPM)

scaling.units: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width Compensation (%)

table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on engine speed and coolant temp.

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation (MAP)

scaling.units: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width Compensation (%)

table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on the manifold absolute pressure.

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation (Accelerator)

scaling.units: Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width Compensation (%)

table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on the accelerator pedal.

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation (IAT)

table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on the accelerator pedal.

table.description: no description

table.name: Hotstart Minimum Enrichment (During Delay and Runtime)
table.description: No Description

table.name: Hotstart Enrichment Decay Delay
table.description: Delay before the Initial Hotstart Enrichment is decayed by the decay step value.

table.name: Hotstart Enrichment Decay Step
table.description: Step value that is decayed from the initial enrichment every time the counter increments.

table.name: Initial Hotstart Enrichment (High)
table.description: Initial High Enrichment. This is applied when BOTH high enrichment thresholds are met.

table.name: Hotstart High Enrichment Activation Threshold (Coolant Temperature)
table.description: Minimum Coolant Temperature threshold for high enrichment.

table.name: Hotstart High Enrichment Activation Threshold (Intake Air Temperature)
table.description: Minimum Intake Air Temperature threshold for high enrichment.

table.name: Initial Hotstart Enrichment (Low)
table.description: Initial Low Enrichment. This is applied when ANY high enrichment threshold is not met AND both low enrichment thresholds are met.

table.name: Hotstart Low Enrichment Activation Threshold (Coolant Temperature)
table.description: Minimum Coolant Temperature threshold for low enrichment.

table.name: Hotstart Low Enrichment Activation Threshold (Intake Air Temperature)
table.description: Minimum Intake Air Temperature threshold for low enrichment.

table.name: Maximum Non-Idle Hotstart Enrichment (Post Delay)
table.description: Maximum Non-Idle enrichment after the Hotstart Maximum Non-Idle Enrichment delay.

table.name: Hotstart Maximum Non-Idle Enrichment Delay
table.description: Delay before Maximum Non-Idle Hotstart Enrichment limit is applied.

table.name: Hotstart Minimum Enrichment Limit Runtime
table.description: Determines how long the minimum enrichment limit is enabled.

table.name: Base Timing
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing A1
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing A2
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing B1
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing B2
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing C
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing D
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing E
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing F
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing G
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing H
table.description: This is the base level of timing. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing Primary Cruise
table.description: This is the base level of timing during non-cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. The actual base timing is also determined by the 'Base Timing Reference Cruise (AVCS related)' table. During a period after initial start (related to AVCS warm-up and other factors), the ECU will calculate Base Timing as primary - min0(primary - reference), with min0 being a function limiting the (primary - reference) result to zero. If you do not want the base timing to follow this behavior, set the primary and reference maps to the same values (separated by cruise/non-cruise). Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing Reference Cruise (AVCS related)
table.description: This is the base level of reference timing during non-cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. The actual base timing is determined as follows: during a period after initial start (related to AVCS warm-up and other factors), the ECU will calculate Base Timing as primary - min0(primary - reference), with min0 being a function limiting the (primary - reference) result to zero. If you do not want the base timing to follow this behavior, set the primary and reference maps to the same values (separated by cruise/non-cruise). Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing Primary Non-Cruise
table.description: This is the base level of timing during non-cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. The actual base timing is also determined by the 'Base Timing Reference Non-Cruise (AVCS related)' table. During a period after initial start (related to AVCS warm-up and other factors), the ECU will calculate Base Timing as primary - min0(primary - reference), with min0 being a function limiting the (primary - reference) result to zero. If you do not want the base timing to follow this behavior, set the primary and reference maps to the same values (separated by cruise/non-cruise).Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing Reference Non-Cruise (AVCS related)
table.description: This is the base level of reference timing during non-cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. The actual base timing is determined as follows: during a period after initial start (related to AVCS warm-up and other factors), the ECU will calculate Base Timing as primary - min0(primary - reference), with min0 being a function limiting the (primary - reference) result to zero. If you do not want the base timing to follow this behavior, set the primary and reference maps to the same values (separated by cruise/non-cruise). Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing Idle
table.description: This is the base timing in idle mode. The 'Base Timing Idle Minimum' value will also be applied if the vehicle speed threshold is met.

table.description: This is the base timing in idle mode when the transmission is not in neutral. Although the map switching between A and B is not entirely understood, it appears to be related to the TGVs.
